rulings.Case Title: RAMIRO HERNANDEZ ET AL VS. SKANSKA USA BUILDING, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hartford Insurance Company Of The Midwests Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Complaint-In-Intervention; Declaration Of Jenna L. Fischer-Reyes In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 27, 2019, line 2. INTERVENOR HARTFORD INSURANCE COMPANY OF THE MIDWEST'S Motion For Leave To File A Complaint-In-Intervention. Complainant-in-Intervention Harford Insurance Company of the Midwest's motion for leave to file a complaint-in-intervention is granted. An employer's workers' compensation insurer has an unconditional statutory right to intervene in an action brought by the employee against a third-party tortfeasor "at any time before trial on the facts." (Lab. Code § 3853; Bailey v. Reliance Ins. Co. (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 449, 454.) Plaintiff did not file timely opposition to the motion. In any event, an insurer's motion for leave to intervene in an employee's third-party action is properly denied only when it is filed after the action has been settled and dismissed. (Aetna Cas. & Sur. Co. v. Superior Court (1993) 20 Cal.App.4th 1502, 1506, 1509 [where insurer's motion for allowance of workers' compensation lien against the proceeds of the settlement was filed "[w]eeks after the complaint was dismissed," it was untimely]; see also Mar v. Sakti Internat. Corp. (1992) 9 Cal.App.4th 1780, 1785 [employer that sought to intervene in injured employee's suit against third parties prior to scheduled trial date has as a matter of law timely applied to intervene, even though employee and defendants had settled].) Here, Hartford's motion was filed on March 4, 2019, before Plaintiff filed its request for dismissal on March 21. (Cf. Andersen v. Barton Memorial Hospital, Inc. (1985) 166 Cal.App.3d 678, 685 n.9 ["'Intervention cannot take place after an action has been dismissed.'"].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Hartford is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BUENOFRIO INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. NIESAR & VESTAL LLP A LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Gerald Niesar, Oscar Escobar, Niesar & Vestal, Llp Notice Of Motion Contesting The Claim Of Privilege Assserted By Non-Party The Central Valley Fund, L.P. Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure, Section 2031.285(D)(1).
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 27 2019, Line 4, Defendant Gerald Niesar, Oscar Escobar, Niesar & Vestal, Llp's Motion Contesting The Claim Of Privilege Assserted By Non-Party The Central Valley Fund, L.P. Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure, Section 2031.285(D)(1). Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID CRUZ VS. BAY AREA GREEN CROSS DISPENSARY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpopenas And For Award Of Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 27 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DAVID CRUZ's Motion To Quash Subpopenas And For Award Of Attorney'S Fees. Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion to Quash Subpoenas is GRANTED and DENIED as follows: Uber Technologies, Inc.: The Motion is GRANTED as to the fourth category (see Opp. 5:27-28). The Motion is GRANTED as to the first and second categories. The Motion is DENIED as to the third category (See Reply 9:12-13). Plaintiff's compensation from this third party is relevant to plaintiff's damages. Woodland Park Apartments: The Motion is GRANTED. The subpoena is quashed in its entirety. Subsequent to the subpoena, plaintiff responded to Special Interrogatory 43. (Reply 6:18-20). Wells Fargo: The Motion is GRANTED. The subpoena is quashed in its entirety. The documents sought are not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Howard Ragland, MD: The Motion is GRANTED and DENIED as follows. The Subpoena is limited to treatment for the injuries plaintiff has put at issue in this action. The request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL VS. EDUARDO C BATO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion (1) To Compel Written Discovery From Defendant Eduardo C. Bato; And (2) Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Eduardo C. Bato
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 27 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion (1) To Compel Written Discovery From Defendant Eduardo C. Bato; And (2) Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Eduardo C. Bato. Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco's unopposed Motion to Compel Defendant Eduardo C. Bato's responses to Plaintiff's first set of Special Interrogatories, Request for Production of Documents, and Request for Admissions is GRANTED. Defendant Bato shall provide verified responses to the discovery within two weeks of notice of this order. Sanctions in the amount of $4,495 in favor of plaintiff against Defendant Bato are due and payable within three weeks of notice of this order. Notice of this order shall be served via overnight mail. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DENTAL FORCE, INC. ET AL VS. BEMDAD BEHNOOD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recover Attorneys Fees And Costs In The Amount Of At Least $12,899.67 From Defendant Bemdad Behnood; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Graham C. Mills In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 27, 2019, line 8. PLAINTIFFS DENTAL FORCE, INC., EVANGELISTA DENTAL GROUP SAN FRANCISCO 450 SUTTER'S Motion To Recover Attorneys Fees And Costs In The Amount Of At Least $12,899.67 From Defendant Bemdad Behnood. Plaintiffs Dental Force, Inc. and Evangelista Dental Group San Francisco 450 Sutter, PC's motion to recover attorneys' fees and costs is denied. Although Defendant Bembab Behnood's anti-SLAPP motion was poorly conceived and supported, both factually and legally, the Court cannot find that it was frivolous (Code Civ. Proc. § 425.16(c)(1)), such that any reasonable attorney would agree that the motion was totally devoid of merit. (See Albanese v. Menounos (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 923, 937 ["Although we rejected [defendant's] contentions on the merits, we cannot say her contentions were frivolous or exceeded the bounds of rational argument."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
